title: Analysis & Discovery
summary: Understand the audience, the gap, and the constraints before designing anything.
primer: Where you figure out the real problem before designing anything. Stakeholder conversations, performance data, audience research, and a written brief. The biggest trap here is jumping to solutions before understanding what's broken.

title: Strategy
summary: Decide what kind of learning experience this needs to be, and why.
primer: The bets you make before you start building: modality, depth, audience priorities, business outcomes. You're not designing yet — you're deciding what kind of training this should even be, and what success looks like.

title: Curriculum
summary: Shape the arc — modules, outcomes, the learner journey.
primer: Shaping the arc. Learning outcomes, the order of modules, what each one teaches, what's assessed. This is where a brief turns into a learner journey.

title: Design
primer: Storyboarding the actual learner experience. Activities, narrative, interactions, assessment, the moment-to-moment of what happens in each module. The point where a training program starts to feel like something specific.

title: Development
summary: Build the assets — slides, video, code, copy.
primer: Production. Building the actual assets — slides, video, scripts, voiceover, code, copy. This is where AI saves the most hours if you know which jobs to give it.

title: Plan
primer: The unglamorous logistics: rollout sequence, comms, registration, scheduling, facilitator prep. The work that decides whether anyone actually shows up — and whether the first cohort goes smoothly or as a near-miss.

title: Execution
summary: Deliver the experience to real learners.
primer: Delivering the experience. Whether it's live, async, or hybrid, this is where training meets reality and the learners' actual context shows up. Good design survives execution; great execution can rescue mediocre design.

title: Measurement
summary: Find out whether it worked, on the dimensions that matter.
primer: Finding out whether it worked, on the dimensions that actually matter. Beyond completion rates and smile sheets: behavior change, business impact, what stuck, what didn't.

title: Improvement
summary: Iterate. Training is a product, not a project.
primer: Treating training as a product, not a project. Reading the signals from execution and measurement, deciding what to iterate, and shipping the next version.

title: Project Management
summary: Keep the work moving — scope, schedule, stakeholders, and risk.
primer: The connective tissue across every other stage. Scope, schedule, stakeholders, risks, decisions. Often the gap between a good design and a design that actually ships on time.
